Hey.

still see them as ready to publish.
Is that true?
No, we didn't publish anything because we were waiting for the images.
Oh, thought we were just using placeholder images until we got the design ones back.
I thought Nate said he just wanted to wait.
Like, give it a week.
See where this is.
I know you said that Slack.
Let's just confirm if there's any more delay than, like, tomorrow.
Let's just put them up with placeholders.
Mm-hmm.
Just to get the ranking going and get the signals and all that that we...

It shouldn't...

Yeah.
Yeah.
Hey, Nate, how's it going?
Good.
How are y'all doing?
Good.
Yeah.
Hopefully, life is good.
I feel like we haven't actually met in a couple of weeks because, well, I know I was out, but I think you were out a couple of times, too.
Yeah, it might have been a month, so it's good to be back.
Yeah.
How are things going for y'all?
No, things are good, for sure.
So, um...
Yeah, that's, things are always moving, so, but it's not a bad thing.
Yeah.
Any, like, new developments on the insurance billing tool or anything exciting that y'all are plotting for Q1 yet?
Or are you still trying to wrap up Q4?
Yeah, I think Q4, we're still in the midst of it, right?
But, um, no, I think, so we actually have a bunch of the people from the U.S.
and Amsterdam this week on the insurance team.
So the whole insurance team is in Amsterdam.
Nice.
So hopefully a lot of progress will be made this week.
I think by the end of next week, we'll actually have TRO pages externally about our insurance product.

Staffing, like we would want to use time to change those to point to the external insurance verification page to try to drive demos there.

So even if that comes at the cost of like not having maybe as many articles or any articles for a week or two or whatever, just to get all of those previous ones changed, that will for sure be a priority once that external page is ready.
And obviously, I think that would be exciting to you, because it's going to become really clear in terms of like value driven from like the amount of demos that we start to grab from those.
Yeah, for sure.
That's a great timing, especially with like the code pages now ramping up for us.
The, as soon as you have those, yeah, if you want to share those with us, we can certainly get started on getting all of those CTAs swapped out for sure on the ones that we have already.
Thank you.
Because I think it's a lot, right?
It's like probably over a hundred articles that are insurance related.
Yeah, think there's a good amount.
I would have to double check the content OS to know for sure how many, but I definitely, we definitely have a good number there.
Yeah, it's like a thousand visits over the last month to our insurance cluster.
I'm looking at the number of articles, yeah, quite a few.
And then anything new, like the billing codes, I'd assume we also send similar CTAs, that should be going forward.
Yeah, for sure.
And then like on the billing code ones, I think like where we're going to get to is, it makes sense for you to do like the most popular ones, but not the long tail.
So it's like where, where I think it makes sense.
And so, yeah, so we need to find out what that cutoff is.
Is that the top 50, the top 75?
I know Jacob, you sent some stuff.

On the data, that's just a line on where the cutoff is there, and then we'll be like, okay, like, these are the ones that we're going to do, don't worry about the long tail, and then from there we'll go back to the other editorial.
Yeah, I think that's right.

What, so the, just a little extra context on that video, I think we'll, like, we have estimates from the few competitors that have done this similar content, so we know what Ahrefs or Sumrush thinks the traffic is, but that can vary, and we'll know pretty quickly because like one type of content all will work pretty similarly, like if Ahrefs estimates 200 traffic, and we're seeing 30, like we can kind of extrapolate that across the whole batch of content pretty confidently.
So we just need to get those early signals.
The good thing, again, is that they're pretty low difficulty, and we have so much dental insurance traffic and content already, we should do really well there.
So that's why I want to just get these live, get that understanding of how far to go.
can I don't it's a lot.
At least 100, it looks like, but we'll understand if we're stretching that further or if we're cutting back, we're seeing lower results.
we just get those started.
I think it'll be, yeah, well, we've got a couple different ranges we can cut off depending on what we see with the early traffic.
Yeah.
So I agree with you.
Like, let's just get them live so we can start to measure.
And, like, I have a plan for the long tail already for, like, the last 650 or 700 in this situation where, like, I have an engineer that's already just going to scrape.

And then you just want to, like, rewrite the daydream ones and put, like, a little different thing in there we'll just, we'll do that to fill out the lower volume ones just so it's more, and it's filled there.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Yeah, so we'll, we were just discussing that before you hopped on.
And then we'll publish those with placeholder, anything that's ready with placeholder images, so they go live and get that clock started.
We'll then, when we have approval over the images here, we'll just replace those and swap those out, but that won't stop.
up anything or change much from Google's perception, more of like what people see when they're on the site.
But we'll those launched and then won't let the images block anymore and just get that clock sorted.
So you're just going to use these three images and like just rotate the three of them.
Is that what you're basically suggesting here?
Is like the placeholder ones?
Oh, just on Rachel, what's the, I think, are these examples or are these just the three that we're going to stick with?
I'm less in tune on the images.
Those were just like the examples that they sent, but we could use those as the placeholders.
It's kind of like your decision, if you want to use those or something different.
I mean, what's the alternative?
Using like similar images to what's on the blog articles currently.
Oh, no, I would do, I would do like these 2D image ones here and you can just rotate them them or just use even one of them.
them.
Yeah.
Like, honestly, like, it's probably smarter just to do one image on all, I don't know, but, yeah, I'd probably just do one of these, and then we'll know they're going back and change those.
Like, and it's like, what is the plan then, like, figuring out the images?
I think what they're doing is they're setting up kind of, like, a image generation flow based on this.
So, like, within one of our pipelines, there's an option where if you feed it in an example image, you can just kind of create, like, related images.
Um, and so they're setting that up for us to then, like, plug these, you know, plug in, like, what the code is, and then it'll generate an image similar to these.
Cool.
All right.
Are these designs, like, in line with what you were hoping for, Nate?
Is there anything, like, that you don't like about these designs that you would like us to switch up, or are these okay with you?
All right.
No, think stylistically it's fine.
Okay.

I think, like, the uniqueness will come as, like, the first one is, like, a bridge, like, and you can kind of see it, so it's, like, it probably, that design makes sense for, like, codes related to a bridge, but, like, would not make sense for codes related to, like, a tooth cleaning, you know?

Mm But, um, whereas, like, I'm sure there's more general images that could fit a bunch of different codes, but, like, this specific one is, like, very specific to, like, a very unique type of thing, is what I would say.
Yeah, and I think that's what the pipeline, the flow that Rachel's talking about will accommodate for, is just generating something that's unique.
But as long as this style is in line with what you were hoping for, then we can go ahead and move forward with that.
Awesome.
And then in terms of the, the number of ones that we're focused on, so we scraped, like, total of 750 URLs.

From Daydream, and then there was another like 64 from Wisdom, but the priority articles that we are working on based on traffic estimates from Sumrush, like some of our tools on the back end, is I believe we have 227 that we're prioritizing, and we're working through those based on traffic volume.
So we're working from top to bottom, ones with the highest traffic volume on down, so that's kind where we're at with that.
Right.
And once we kind of reach the end of these like top priority pages, we can, you know, continue to reevaluate as we get more traffic indicators from the ones that we're publishing.

So, you know, if we get to the last hundred or something, and it's not proving to be as valuable as we thought, we can always send you the list of the ones that we haven't done yet, so you know what to hand off to your engineer team.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
you.
Yeah, yeah, I mean, like, essentially, like, my, like, I had an engineer already rip all 750s well and rewrite them.
I think, like, your ones are better quality, for sure.
So, like, essentially, it's just going to be, like, how we do the long tail of them.
So, yeah.
And I can, where does it make sense to cut, so.
Yeah, it makes sense.
Okay, cool.
um, other than that, I think the, the only other thing on our agenda for today was just the, uh, CSAT survey that Andy sent over.
Obviously, you're probably pretty busy with the whole team being in Amsterdam this week.
But if you have time and want to share some feedback, I think she said it would only take, like, five minutes or something.
We'd love to get any feedback you have for us that we can do better or more for you.
So, but, yeah, other than that, I don't think we had anything else.
there anything that you need from us, though?
Like, especially as you guys are wrapping
Oh, Jacob, did you have something?
Just two quick notes.

The insurance articles total before the dental codes is like 65, Nate, just to, so we are all, like, I'm sure there might be a couple that are outside of those, but that's what it says in Airtable, so when we're thinking about replacing those CTAs, that's kind of the volume.
I don't know what the timeline would spend, but that could easily be done.
I would think that could be done in a week.
And then the only thing we want to think about with sort of doing all the individual codes is probably a landing page listing each code as that starts to get populated.

don't think it's important today, but having some sort of design to have one page that lists them all and then links over to them and then probably put that into a site footer would be my guess to spread that.
I think you just can make that findable, so just want to put that on our radar so we have...
I already have a designer build it, so we have that as well.
Nate, we are all synced up.
I love it.
All right.
Cool.
Thanks, man.
I appreciate
Awesome.
Anything else that you need for us right now, Nate?
No, this is perfect.
Thank you.
All right.
Sounds good.
Well, enjoy the team in Amsterdam and have a good rest of your week.
Bye, guys.
See ya.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on content publishing strategy and upcoming priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Publish billing codes with placeholders immediately.** This unblocks SEO signals and data collection, which will inform the strategy for the remaining \~500 long-tail articles.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
  - [**Repurpose existing insurance content.** Once Teero's new insurance page launches next week, update CTAs on \~65 articles with \~1,000 monthly visits to drive demos.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**Prioritize high-volume codes.** GrowthX will publish the top 227 codes, while Nate's team will use a lower-quality, engineered scrape to cover the remaining long-tail.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [**Image generation is in progress.** A new pipeline will create unique, code-specific images to replace the placeholders, ensuring visual relevance.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)

## Topics

### Billing Code Publishing Strategy

  - [**Problem:** Publishing is blocked by a wait for custom images.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=8.0)
  - [**Solution:** Publish all ready codes with placeholders immediately to begin collecting SEO signals (ranking, traffic).](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This establishes a baseline to measure performance against traffic estimates from Ahrefs/Semrush, informing the strategy for the remaining codes.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
  - [**Placeholder Details:** Use one of the 2D example images provided by Rachel across all articles.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=431.0)
  - [**Image Generation:** A new pipeline is being built to generate unique, code-specific images (e.g., a "bridge" image for a bridge code) to replace placeholders.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)

### Billing Code Content Prioritization

  - [**Total Scraped:** \~814 codes (750 from Daydream, 64 from Wisdom).](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [**GrowthX Focus:** The top 227 codes, prioritized by estimated traffic volume.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [**Long-Tail Strategy:** Nate's team will use a lower-quality, engineered scrape/rewrite for the remaining \~500 codes.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
  - [**Decision Point:** GrowthX will share the list of unwritten codes to define the handoff point between the two teams.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)

### Insurance Content Repurposing

  - [**Trigger:** Teero's new external insurance verification page launches next week.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**Action:** Update CTAs on \~65 existing insurance articles to link to the new page.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**Goal:** Drive demos directly from high-intent content.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**Impact:** This cluster currently receives \~1,000 visits/month, creating a significant opportunity for demo generation.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)

### Code Indexing & Discovery

  - [**Need:** A central landing page to list all individual code articles.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=746.0)
  - [**Purpose:** Improve user discovery and internal linking for SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=746.0)
  - [**Status:** Nate confirmed a designer has already built this page, aligning with GrowthX's plan.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=746.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
      - [Publish all ready billing codes using a single placeholder image.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
      - [Update CTAs on \~65 insurance articles to link to the new insurance page (post-launch).](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
      - [Share the list of unwritten codes with Nate to define the handoff point.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
  - [**Nate:**](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
      - [Share the new insurance page URL with GrowthX upon launch.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**All:**](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)
      - [Complete the 5-minute CSAT survey sent by Andy.](https://fathom.video/share/pgShHkeB-a72YbLyAhy6ZUkb5dz4E_Lz?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)

action_items.description: Publish code pages w/ placeholder image; replace w/ custom images when ready
